UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user profile within the AgriGrow AI application, linking all farm-related data to a specific farmer. Authentication is handled externally.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity. This ID is typically provided by the external authentication system.

email.description: The user's email address, used for contact and potentially notifications. (Optional)

phoneNumber.description: The user's primary phone number, primarily used for OTP authentication and communication.

preferredLanguage.description: The user's preferred language for the application interface and communications (e.g., 'en', 'hi', 'es').

registrationDate.description: The date and time when the user registered for the AgriGrow AI application.

CropType.title: CropType
CropType.description: Represents a type of crop that can be cultivated on fields, providing a standardized list for selection.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CropType entity.

name.description: The common name of the crop (e.g., 'Corn', 'Wheat', 'Soybean').

description.description: A brief description of the crop type and its characteristics. (Optional)

Field.title: Field
Field.description: Represents an agricultural field managed by a user, tracking its characteristics and current crop details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Field entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns this field. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Field)

name.description: A user-friendly name for the field (e.g., 'North Field 1', 'Section A').

areaAmount.description: The numerical value of the field's area.

areaUnit.description: The unit of measurement for the field's area (e.g., 'acres', 'hectares').

currentCropTypeId.description: Reference to the CropType currently planted or most recently planted in this field. (Relationship: CropType 1:N Field)

soilPH.description: The measured pH level of the soil in the field.

plantingDate.description: The date when the current crop was planted in this field.

lastHarvestDate.description: The date when the last harvest occurred for the current crop. (Optional)

locationDescription.description: A textual description or coordinate string of the field's geographical location. (Optional)

description.description: Any additional notes or description for the field. (Optional)

YieldHistory.title: YieldHistory
YieldHistory.description: Records historical yield data for a specific crop harvested from a particular field.

id.description: Unique identifier for the YieldHistory entity.

fieldId.description: Reference to the Field where the crop was harvested. (Relationship: Field 1:N YieldHistory)

cropTypeId.description: Reference to the CropType that was harvested. (Relationship: CropType 1:N YieldHistory)

harvestDate.description: The date when the crop was harvested.

yieldUnit.description: The unit of measurement for the harvested yield (e.g., 'kg', 'bushels', 'tons/acre').

notes.description: Any additional notes regarding this harvest record. (Optional)

YieldForecast.title: YieldForecast
YieldForecast.description: Stores predictive yield estimates generated by AI models for specific fields and crops.

id.description: Unique identifier for the YieldForecast entity.

fieldId.description: Reference to the Field for which the forecast is made. (Relationship: Field 1:N YieldForecast)

cropTypeId.description: Reference to the CropType for which the forecast is made. (Relationship: CropType 1:N YieldForecast)

forecastDate.description: The date and time when this yield forecast was generated.

predictedHarvestDate.description: The estimated future harvest date for which the yield is predicted.

predictedYieldUnit.description: The unit of measurement for the predicted yield (e.g., 'kg', 'bushels', 'tons/acre').

confidenceScore.description: A score indicating the confidence level of the AI model's prediction (e.g., 0-100).

modelUsed.description: The AI model used to generate this forecast (e.g., 'Gemini 1.5 Pro').

explanation.description: An explanation from the AI detailing the reasoning behind the yield prediction (Explainable AI Insight).

GenerativeRecommendation.title: GenerativeRecommendation
GenerativeRecommendation.description: Contains actionable advice generated by AI based on field data, forecasts, and other relevant information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GenerativeRecommendation entity.

fieldId.description: Reference to the Field for which the recommendation is provided. (Relationship: Field 1:N GenerativeRecommendation)

recommendationText.description: The detailed actionable advice provided by the AI (e.g., 'Apply 50kg lime/acre').

recommendationType.description: The category of the recommendation (e.g., 'Fertilizer', 'Irrigation', 'Pest Control', 'Planting').

generatedDate.description: The date and time when this recommendation was generated.

sourceForecastId.description: Reference to the YieldForecast that this recommendation is based on. (Optional) (Relationship: YieldForecast 1:N GenerativeRecommendation)

status.description: The current status of the recommendation (e.g., 'Pending', 'Applied', 'Dismissed').

appliedDate.description: The date and time when the user marked the recommendation as applied. (Optional)

notes.description: User-added notes or observations about applying the recommendation. (Optional)

MeteorologicalData.title: MeteorologicalData
MeteorologicalData.description: Stores real-time and historical meteorological data for a specific field location, integrated from external weather services.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MeteorologicalData entity.

fieldId.description: Reference to the Field for which this weather data is relevant. (Relationship: Field 1:N MeteorologicalData)

timestamp.description: The date and time when this meteorological data was recorded.

temperatureCelsius.description: The air temperature in degrees Celsius.

humidityPercentage.description: The relative humidity as a percentage.

precipitationMM.description: The amount of precipitation in millimeters. (Optional)

windSpeedKPH.description: The wind speed in kilometers per hour. (Optional)

weatherCondition.description: A brief description of the weather condition (e.g., 'Clear', 'Partly Cloudy', 'Rain').

source.description: The source of the meteorological data (e.g., 'OpenWeatherMap').

WeatherAlert.title: WeatherAlert
WeatherAlert.description: Records dynamic alerts generated based on live meteorological data for specific fields.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WeatherAlert entity.

fieldId.description: Reference to the Field to which this alert applies. (Relationship: Field 1:N WeatherAlert)

alertType.description: The type of weather alert (e.g., 'Frost Warning', 'Irrigation Needed', 'Heatwave Advisory').

message.description: The full message content of the alert.

alertTimestamp.description: The date and time when the alert was issued.

isDismissed.description: Indicates whether the user has dismissed this alert.

triggerCondition.description: The specific meteorological condition that triggered this alert (e.g., 'Temp below 0C', 'Soil moisture low').

ChatSession.title: ChatSession
ChatSession.description: Represents a single conversation session between a user and the AI Farm Assistant (Agri-Bot).

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChatSession entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile participating in this chat session. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ChatSession)

startTime.description: The date and time when the chat session started.

endTime.description: The date and time when the chat session ended. (Optional)

languageCode.description: The language code used for this chat session (e.g., 'en' for English, 'es' for Spanish).

ChatMessage.title: ChatMessage
ChatMessage.description: Stores individual messages within a chat session with the AI Farm Assistant (Agri-Bot).

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChatMessage entity.

chatSessionId.description: Reference to the ChatSession this message belongs to. (Relationship: ChatSession 1:N ChatMessage)

timestamp.description: The date and time when the message was sent.

senderType.description: Indicates who sent the message ('User' or 'Agri-Bot').

messageContent.description: The textual content of the message.

DiseaseDetectionRecord.title: DiseaseDetectionRecord
DiseaseDetectionRecord.description: Records instances of disease or pest detection from user-submitted photos, including identified issues and treatment plans.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DiseaseDetectionRecord entity.

fieldId.description: Reference to the Field where the photo was taken and the disease/pest detected. (Relationship: Field 1:N DiseaseDetectionRecord)

cropTypeId.description: Reference to the CropType affected by the detected disease or pest. (Relationship: CropType 1:N DiseaseDetectionRecord)

timestamp.description: The date and time when the disease detection was performed or photo submitted.

imageUrl.description: URL to the uploaded image used for detection.

detectedDisease.description: The name of the detected disease or pest (e.g., 'Powdery Mildew', 'Aphids Infestation').

confidenceScore.description: A score (0-1) indicating the AI's confidence in the detection result.

treatmentPlan.description: The multi-part treatment plan recommended by the AI for the detected issue.

status.description: The current status of the detection record (e.g., 'Detected', 'Treatment Applied', 'Monitoring').

notes.description: Any additional user notes or observations regarding the disease/pest or treatment. (Optional)

SoilAnalysisReport.title: SoilAnalysisReport
SoilAnalysisReport.description: Stores extracted metrics from scanned physical soil lab reports, providing detailed soil chemistry data for a field.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SoilAnalysisReport entity.

fieldId.description: Reference to the Field for which the soil report was scanned. (Relationship: Field 1:N SoilAnalysisReport)

scanDate.description: The date and time when the physical soil report was scanned and processed.

reportDocumentUrl.description: URL to the stored scanned document image or PDF of the soil report. (Optional)

extractedPH.description: The pH value extracted from the soil report.

extractedNitrogenPercentage.description: The Nitrogen (N) percentage extracted from the soil report.

extractedPhosphorusPercentage.description: The Phosphorus (P) percentage extracted from the soil report.

extractedPotassiumPercentage.description: The Potassium (K) percentage extracted from the soil report.

extractedMoisturePercentage.description: The soil moisture percentage extracted from the soil report.

notes.description: Any additional notes or observations related to the soil analysis report. (Optional)

definition.description: Stores individual user profile data. The document's ID (which is also the 'id' field within the document) must match the Firebase Authentication UID (`request.auth.uid`).

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: A global, read-only collection providing a standardized list of crop types available in the system.

params.name: cropTypeId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific crop type.

definition.description: Represents an agricultural field owned by a specific user. Includes denormalized `ownerId` (matching `userId` from path) for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns this field.

params.name: fieldId
params.description: The unique identifier for the field.

definition.description: Records historical yield data for a specific crop from a particular field. Includes denormalized `ownerId` (from parent Field) for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns the parent field.

params.name: fieldId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent field.

params.name: yieldHistoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific yield history record.

definition.description: Stores predictive yield estimates generated by AI models for specific fields. Includes denormalized `ownerId` (from parent Field) for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns the parent field.

params.name: fieldId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent field.

params.name: yieldForecastId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific yield forecast record.

definition.description: Contains actionable advice generated by AI for a specific field. Includes denormalized `ownerId` (from parent Field) for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns the parent field.

params.name: fieldId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent field.

params.name: recommendationId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific generative recommendation.

params.name: userId

params.name: fieldId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent field.

params.name: dataId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific meteorological data record.

params.name: userId

params.name: alertId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific weather alert.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for AgriGrow AI is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and clear Access Modeling, directly addressing the core design principles and user request for live updates on the dashboard.User-specific data, such as `UserProfile`, `Field` records, `ChatSession`s, and all related sub-entities, are nested under a top-level `/users/{userId}` collection. This path-based ownership (`/users/{userId}/fields/{fieldId}`) is the primary mechanism for securing user data, ensuring that users can only access their own records.The `CropType` entity, representing a global, standardized list, resides in its own top-level collection (`/cropTypes/{cropTypeId}`). This segregation clearly defines its different security posture (read-only for all authenticated users) compared to private user data, simplifying security rules and maintaining a homogeneous security profile within the collection.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
To achieve critical Authorization Independence, a key strategy is the denormalization of the `ownerId` field into every document within user-owned collections and their subcollections. Specifically:
1.  **UserProfile (`/users/{userId}`):** The document's `id` field will match the `userId` in the path (which is `request.auth.uid`). Rules can directly verify `resource.data.id == request.auth.uid`.
2.  **Field (`/users/{userId}/fields/{fieldId}`):** Each `Field` document will include an explicit `ownerId` field, storing the `userId` from its path. This allows security rules to check `resource.data.ownerId == request.auth.uid` without needing to perform a `get()` operation on the parent `UserProfile`.
3.  **All Field Subcollections:** Documents in `YieldHistory`, `YieldForecast`, `GenerativeRecommendation`, `MeteorologicalData`, `WeatherAlert`, `DiseaseDetectionRecord`, and `SoilAnalysisReport` (all nested under `/users/{userId}/fields/{fieldId}/...`) will *also* denormalize the `ownerId` from their parent `Field` document. This ensures that rules for any document in these subcollections can perform an atomic check `resource.data.ownerId == request.auth.uid`, eliminating all hierarchical authorization dependencies and enabling atomic operations.
4.  **ChatSession (`/users/{userId}/chatSessions/{chatSessionId}`):** Similar to `Field`, each `ChatSession` document will contain an `ownerId` field matching the `userId` from its path.
5.  **ChatMessage (`/users/{userId}/chatSessions/{chatSessionId}/messages/{messageId}`):** Each `ChatMessage` document will denormalize the `ownerId` from its parent `ChatSession` for direct, independent authorization checks.

This pervasive denormalization strategy ensures that security rules are simple, robust, and easily debuggable, as authorization logic for any document can be resolved by inspecting the document itself, preventing expensive and complex `get()` calls in rules.

**Support for QAPs (Queries as Authorization Policies):**
The structural design inherently supports Query Authorization Policies (QAPs) by leveraging path-based ownership. When a user queries `/users/{request.auth.uid}/fields`, Firestore's security rules can ensure that the `userId` wildcard matches `request.auth.uid`, thus implicitly filtering the results to only the current user's fields. For subcollections, like `/users/{request.auth.uid}/fields/{fieldId}/yieldHistory`, the path itself enforces the user's ownership of the parent field. The denormalized `ownerId` within each document provides an additional, explicit layer for rules to enforce ownership during `list` operations, guaranteeing that only authorized data is returned, eliminating the need for client-side filtering and ensuring data integrity and security at the database level. For instance, a rule can be `allow list: if request.auth.uid == request.path[1] && resource.data.ownerId == request.auth.uid;` (though for the hierarchical structure, `request.path[1]` usually suffices, the denormalized `ownerId` adds resilience).

The user's request for 'live updates' on their dashboard when fields are added or deleted is perfectly supported by this structure. The application can subscribe to real-time updates on `/users/{request.auth.uid}/fields` and its subcollections, allowing the UI to reflect changes instantly without complex logic or further security implications.
